---
title: "Computational Reconstruction of Zhang's Marxian Formalization"
description: "Research project: implementing Zhang's game-theoretic, probabilistic, and cybernetic extensions to Marxian economics — toward a publishable computational companion to Zhang 2023."
---

![](assets/images/cover.png){.cover-banner fig-alt="Zhang's Marxian Formalization — Research Project"}

## About this research project

This is a **research project**, not a course. There is no syllabus, no fixed pacing, no exercises. The goal is a body of work — reading notes, working papers, an open-source library, and (if the work warrants) a methodology paper for *World Review of Political Economy* — that constitutes a computationally rigorous companion to Zhang Xian's 2023 *WRPE* paper "The Formalization of Marx's Economics."

The project will be expanded after the seminar between Controversies 5 and 6 in [The Calculation Course](/courses/the_calculation_course/), once there are real views about what Zhang's positive program warrants.

For the full rationale, planned scope, and prospective endpoints, see the placeholder in the Calculation Course at [`research-marxian-formalization/placeholder.qmd`](/courses/the_calculation_course/research-marxian-formalization/placeholder.html).

## Prerequisites

Complete all 10 weeks and all 7 controversies of [The Calculation Course](/courses/the_calculation_course/), with particular attention to:

- The seminar on the Chinese critique of the Japanese School (between Controversies 5 and 6)
- Controversy 3 (Transformation Problem) and Controversy 5 (Okishio's Theorem)
- Zhang, "The Formalization of Marx's Economics: A Summary Attempt Taking as an Example the First Volume of Capital" (*WRPE* 14(1), 2023)

## Planned scope

Eight working areas tracking Zhang's 2023 Vol I formalization. Each is a research thread, not a teaching unit:

1. Value theory formalization (the mapping f: A → w, two senses of SNLT, labor complexity)
2. Competition as a dynamic game (prisoner's-dilemma payoffs, mixed strategies, repeated games)
3. Realization uncertainty and crisis probability (distribution F(s) over outcomes, means-of-payment)
4. The labor process as a cybernetic system (Zhang's y = x · S/(1−SR) equation)
5. Unemployment dynamics (Zhang's differential equation, empirical calibration)
6. Capital accumulation and the LTRPF (game-theoretic route; comparison with Okishio and TSSI)
7. Primitive accumulation / deprivation (Zhang's stochastic deprivation function)
8. Unified computational framework (single calibrated economy, library backend)

## Prospective endpoints

1. A methodology paper submitted to *WRPE* — co-authored with Zhang if he is interested.
2. An open-source library (`marxecon` or similar) implementing Morishima-Okishio, TSSI, and Zhang as alternative backends against a shared economy object.
3. A direct contribution to the Cockshott / Dapprich planning tradition.

*This page will be expanded as the project proceeds.*
